gaps.description: Bill of Rights is an empty scaffold. No Articles I–VI have been defined. Arbiter (#190) cannot enforce a scope perimeter without human-authored constitutional content. Every scope query Arbiter performs is against a document whose only meaningful statement is its own incompleteness. The entire governance chain — Arbiter scope gate → Nomos MSP/genome gate → Action Proposal authorization — rests on a constitutional vacuum.
gaps.mission_ref: BoR Article I — Scope of Enterprise (scaffold, awaiting human authorship)

gaps.description: Mission Statement Protocol contains zero operational rules. Nomos (#200) operates in default-permissive mode, meaning every Action Proposal passes the MSP + genome compliance gate regardless of content. The MSP is the DIO's operational constitution — its emptiness means there are no machine-actionable safety invariants, constraints, or objectives. Critical incident goals dispatched in this session reference 'MSP §incident-response' which does not exist.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP — Rules section (bootstrap seed, no rules)

gaps.description: Five critical incident response goals were dispatched referencing 'MSP §incident-response'. That section does not exist — the MSP has zero rules, zero sections. The goals are unmoored from any constitutional or operational authority. Nomos cannot evaluate whether these goals' Action Proposals comply with mission constraints because there are no mission constraints to evaluate against.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP — Nomos adjudication requirement (AP must pass MSP + genome compliance gate)

gaps.description: All 25 organs are experiencing continuous health-state oscillation (ALIVE ↔ DEGRADED every ~30 seconds). Spine is generating missed_pong → pong_recovered transitions in tight loops across the entire organism. This is systemic organ instability — not isolated failures. The pattern covers Receptor, Thalamus, Senate, Nomos, Arbiter, Syntra, Lobe, Soul, Hippocampus, Minder, Radiant, Vectr, Phi, Axon, Engram, GitSync, Promote, MCP-Gateway, Glia, Sourcegraph, MCP-Router, Vigil, Cerberus, ModelBroker, Graph, and SafeVault. No genome document exists to define organ-specific health thresholds or escalation rules.

SafeVault backups have failed or partially failed across 6 of the last 7 recorded runs (April 27–May 3). Most recent failure (May 3): sqlite3 .backup blocked indefinitely by two zombie processes from May 2 still alive ~25 hours later, causing today's process to be killed. NAS mount has been unavailable since at least April 7. Script bugs (unbound variable ORGAN_SQLITE_DBS on April 30) caused outright failure with no retry. There is no MSP rule defining backup success criteria, retry policy, or zombie process remediation.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP — Operational continuity (implicit in DIO operational constitution purpose)

gaps.description: Radiant's dream cycle is disabled and has never run. `scheduled: 'disabled'`, `health: 'never_run'`, `last_dream: null`. Memory consolidation (Phase 2) is not occurring. Context freshness triage is not occurring. 27 of 96 memories lack embeddings (72% coverage). The dream cycle is the mechanism that prevents context bloat and maintains memory coherence. No genome document exists to define Radiant's operational requirements or alert thresholds for dream cycle lapse.
gaps.mission_ref: MSP — Genome References (Radiant organ-specific operating rules)

gaps.description: Hippocampus records zero conversations despite Minder holding 40+ card entries per peer and Radiant containing extensive session-completion memories. The DIO is accumulating interaction-derived memory (Minder observations, Radiant consolidated memories) with no conversational context preservation. Without BoR Article II, there is no constraint on what conversational data may or may not be retained. With Hippocampus effectively offline, the DIO lacks episodic memory integrity.
gaps.mission_ref: BoR Article II — Prohibited Activities (scaffold, awaiting human authorship)

raw_responses:
```json
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "REL-001",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights scaffold contains no substantive articles, leaving the relational principles that should govern all person-attention coverage (including how authority is reserved, how human intent is validated, and what constitutes prohibited interaction patterns) completely undefined. Without these constitutional relation rules, the DIO cannot align its Minder observations, Hippocampus conversation tracking, or HOM notification loops to any binding relational guardrails.",
      "msp_bor_reference": "BoR Articles I–VI (all currently 'To be defined by human principal'); MSP 'Relationship to the Bill of Rights' clause which states MSP is strictly subordinate to BoR.",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": 10
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "REL-002",
      "description": "The MSP amendment protocol requires a HOM notification to the 'human-principal' mailbox and a human approval action (POST /amend/review with decision=approve), yet the peer registry contains no entry for a human-principal role, no dedicated attention channel for that mailbox, and no recent conversation records in Hippocampus to track such relational approvals. The person-attention coverage lacks any operational feedback loop for the human principal’s decision-making, leaving the amendment safeguard purely declarative.",
      "msp_bor_reference": "MSP 'Amendment Protocol' section: '…an HOM notification is emitted to the human-principal mailbox. Activation requires a POST /amend/review with decision=approve.'",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": 9
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "REL-003",
      "description": "The Minder contains extensive deductive observations about Leon’s preferences (e.g., requiring intent clarification before infrastructure changes, opposing feature creep, expecting AI to learn governance branch impacts), but the person-attention coverage shows no active relational loop for confirming these preferences or capturing Leon’s current intent. The Hippocampus recent conversations array is empty, indicating a lack of real-time relational data, which risks the system acting on stale or misattributed preferences without constitutional guidance on how human reservations of authority should be exercised.",
      "msp_bor_reference": "BoR Article IV ('Human Authority Reservation' – undefined but implicitly required for attention alignment); MSP 'Relationship to the Bill of Rights' subordinate clause.",
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": 7
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "REL-004",
      "description": "The peer registry includes a persona 'Claude' treated as an observation target with 253 observations and 40 card entries, yet neither the MSP nor the BoR defines the relational standing of AI personas, how attention should be differentiated from human peers, or whether inferring constraints from a persona is constitutionally permissible. This lack of relational boundary risks misallocation of attention toward persona-derived constraints without human-authorised principles.",
      "msp_bor_reference": "BoR Article II ('Prohibited Activities' – undefined); MSP 'Genome References' and 'Rules' sections provide no organ-specific relational rules for persona interaction.",
      "criticality": "low",
      "urgency": "low",
      "impact": "medium",
      "priority_score": 4
    }
  ]
}
```

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "urn:llm-ops:constitutional-gap:bor-identity-scaffold-empty",
      "title": "Bill of Rights Identity Articles Entirely Undefined",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights v1.0.0-seed contains no substantive identity provisions. All six articles (Scope of Enterprise, Prohibited Activities, Core Identity, Human Authority Reservation, Amendment Protocol, Interpretation Rules) remain marked '[To be defined by human principal]'. This means the organism has no constitutional definition of what it IS or what it must NEVER do. The recent critical incident response goals (e.g., urn:llm-ops:goal:phase2-crit-0-pfclqq through -4-oxsk7z) are operating without any BoR identity perimeter. Any action taken under these goals—such as emergency resource allocation, autonomous decision-making, or communication with external systems—could violate a future BoR prohibition or exceed an intended scope, creating latent constitutional conflict. The BoR scaffold is currently non-functional as a constitutional instrument, leaving the organism's identity unconstrained.",

        "Article I — Scope of Enterprise",
        "Article II — Prohibited Activities",
